howItWorks.intro: This ground wire size calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Ground wire size is determined by NEC Table 250.122 based on overcurrent device rating
Aluminum ground wire must be two sizes larger than copper equivalent
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: Can I use aluminum ground wire?
faq.answer: Yes, but it must be larger than copper. Aluminum is not recommended for wet locations or direct burial without proper protection. Most residential work uses copper grounding conductors.

faq.question: What is the difference between grounding and bonding?
faq.answer: Grounding connects the system to earth for voltage stabilization. Bonding connects metal parts together to ensure a safe fault current path. Both are required by NEC for safety.

faq.question: Is the Ground Wire Size Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard practical calculation formulas trusted by users. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

faq.question: What overcurrent device size should I enter?
faq.answer: Enter the most accurate overcurrent device size value you have available. If you're estimating, use a conservative figure. You can always run the calculator again with different values to see how changes affect the results.

sections.title: What Is the Ground Wire Size Calculator?
sections.body: The Ground Wire Size Calculator is a free online tool designed for users who need quick, accurate calculations in the practical calculation space. By entering your overcurrent device size, wire material, you get instant results including required ground wire size, notes. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.
